guildrunBeginnerGuide.title: Guildrun Beginner Guide
guildrunBeginnerGuide.intro: Guildrun is a single-player PvE roguelike autobattler built around recruiting heroes, preparing a team and adapting to random shop choices. The full game is planned to feature 25 heroes, 75 specialization paths, 180 specializations, more than 100 items and more than 300 relics.

steps.title: Recruit a Balanced Starting Team
steps.description: Begin with heroes that cover three basic jobs: a durable frontline, a dependable damage dealer and a support or utility unit. Avoid filling every slot with fragile damage heroes, because unprotected carries can be defeated before their damage becomes useful.
steps.priority: Frontline first, dependable damage second, utility third
steps.mistake: Buying every hero offered without considering the team role it fills

steps.title: Check the Shop Before Committing
steps.description: Review the full shop before spending resources. Compare new recruits, duplicate upgrades, items and long-term specialization options, then choose the purchase that improves the entire team rather than a single weak unit.
steps.priority: Buy immediate power before speculative upgrades
steps.mistake: Using repeated rerolls early and leaving too few resources for upgrades

steps.title: Build Around the Strongest Offers
steps.description: Do not force the same composition every run. Use the heroes, relics and items that appear naturally, then change direction when the shop repeatedly offers a stronger class combination or shared effect.
steps.priority: Flexible upgrades and shared team effects
steps.mistake: Holding resources for one exact hero, item or specialization

steps.title: Choose Specializations With a Clear Purpose
steps.description: Guildrun heroes can develop through multiple specialization paths. Select paths that strengthen the hero current job, such as survivability for a frontline unit, consistent damage for a carry or team-wide value for a support.
steps.priority: Complete one useful role before spreading upgrades across several roles
steps.mistake: Choosing unrelated bonuses that do not support the hero position or team

steps.title: Equip Items and Relics Before Combat
steps.description: Place defensive equipment on heroes that absorb the opening attacks and offensive equipment on heroes that remain alive long enough to use it. Relics should support the whole composition, resource plan or specialization direction whenever possible.
steps.priority: Survival for the frontline and scaling for the main carry
steps.mistake: Giving rare items to a hero that does not benefit from their effect

steps.title: Prepare Positioning and Reserve Units
steps.description: Arrange durable heroes where they can intercept pressure and keep important damage or support heroes protected. Use reserve units as replacements, future specialization options or answers to fights where the current lineup performs poorly.
steps.mistake: Using the same formation against every encounter

steps.title: Learn From the First Completed Run
steps.description: After each battle, identify whether the team lacked damage, durability or synergy. Spend Shards and other run resources on upgrades that improve future consistency, then progress toward higher difficulties and Endless mode after the basic loop feels stable.
steps.mistake: Changing the entire team after one bad fight instead of fixing its weakest role

quickTips: Stabilize a frontline before chasing expensive damage upgrades.
Compare every shop offer against your whole team, not just one weak hero.
Keep enough resources for important rerolls instead of spending early.
Use reserve heroes to answer difficult encounters instead of forcing one formation.

guildrunDemoAndRelease.title: Guildrun Demo and Release Date
guildrunDemoAndRelease.intro: Players can download the standalone Guildrun Demo through Steam and experience the core roguelike autobattler loop before the full release. The complete version is planned to expand the roster, progression systems, difficulty options and multiplayer features.

cards.name: Free Demo
cards.description: The Guildrun Demo is a free standalone download that introduces hero recruitment, shop decisions, upgrades, specializations, items, relics and automatic combat.

cards.name: Full-Game Release Window
cards.description: The full version of Guildrun is targeting a 2027 release. The main Steam page can be followed or wishlisted for future release announcements.

cards.name: Platform
cards.description: Both the demo and the main Guildrun store listing are available through Steam.

cards.name: Planned Hero Roster
cards.description: The full game is planned around a roster of 25 recruitable heroes that can be developed into different team roles.

cards.name: Specialization Content
cards.description: Heroes can be developed through 75 specialization paths containing 180 specialization choices, allowing teams to change direction during a run.

cards.name: Items and Relics
cards.description: Runs are shaped by more than 100 items and more than 300 relics that modify hero performance, team effects and build direction.

cards.name: Difficulty and Endless Mode
cards.description: Guildrun is planned to include eight difficulty levels and an Endless mode for players who want longer and more demanding runs.

cards.name: Co-op
cards.description: Co-op is included in the game planned development scope rather than the current single-player demo experience.

guildrunHeroesTierList.title: Guildrun Heroes Tier List
guildrunHeroesTierList.intro: Guildrun is designed around 25 heroes and 75 specialization paths, so a hero value depends on more than starting damage. This tier structure highlights the hero profiles that provide the most consistent results when shop offers, relics and team combinations change between runs.

tiers.label: Run-Defining Heroes
tiers.profile: Flexible carries, durable frontline anchors and supports with strong team-wide effects
tiers.reason: These heroes contribute immediately, scale through several specialization paths and remain useful even when ideal items do not appear.
tiers.items: Items that improve the hero existing role without requiring a narrow combination
tiers.fit: Can become the main carry, primary tank or central support in several compositions

tiers.label: Strong and Consistent Heroes
tiers.profile: Dependable damage dealers, secondary frontliners and focused support heroes
tiers.reason: These heroes perform well in most runs but usually need suitable positioning, one strong specialization path or a compatible frontline.
tiers.items: Role-specific items that improve damage consistency, survivability or support uptime
tiers.fit: Excellent alongside an S-tier anchor or as the second major piece of a balanced team

tiers.label: Build-Dependent Heroes
tiers.profile: Heroes that become powerful with the correct relics, items, shared effects or specialization sequence
tiers.reason: Their ceiling can be high, but their early performance is less consistent and they may consume too many resources before the build becomes stable.
tiers.items: Specific items that activate the hero main scaling mechanic
tiers.fit: Best used when the shop repeatedly supports the same build direction

tiers.label: Situational Heroes
tiers.profile: Narrow counters, fragile specialists and heroes that require several exact upgrades
tiers.reason: These heroes can solve particular encounters but are harder to use during a first run because their strength depends on precise support.
tiers.items: Highly specialized equipment that fixes a major weakness or enables a specific interaction
tiers.fit: Reserve unit, temporary specialist or final piece of an already established composition

tiers.label: How Each Hero Is Scored
tiers.profile: Every hero is compared across the same six categories
tiers.reason: A useful tier list measures complete run performance rather than one successful battle.
tiers.items: Item dependence is treated as a weakness when a hero needs several exact drops
tiers.fit: Heroes rank higher when they work in multiple formations and specialization routes

guildrunBestTeamBuilds.title: Guildrun Best Team Builds
guildrunBestTeamBuilds.intro: Guildrun team building combines hero roles, specialization paths, items, relics, positioning and shared effects. These compositions focus on repeatable structures rather than requiring one exact set of shop choices.

builds.name: Balanced First-Run Team
builds.positioning: Place the most durable hero in the exposed position, keep the main carry protected and position the support where it can survive long enough to provide value.
builds.items: Defensive items go to the frontline, consistent damage items go to the carry and utility items go to the support.
builds.relics: Choose relics that improve the whole team or provide dependable resources between battles.
builds.upgrade: Stabilize the frontline first, then improve carry damage and finally add utility.
builds.alternatives: Replace missing roles with any hero whose specialization can perform the same job.

builds.name: Lovers Shared-Effect Build
builds.positioning: Keep the Lovers pair protected and avoid exposing both linked heroes to the same opening pressure.
builds.items: Give survival tools to the more vulnerable member and scaling items to the hero providing the composition main damage.
builds.relics: Prioritize relics that improve shared effects, survivability or the entire active team.
builds.upgrade: Activate the shared effect, secure a frontline and then invest in the stronger scaling member of the pair.
builds.alternatives: Transition into a balanced team if the required pair or supporting upgrades stop appearing.

builds.name: Single-Carry Scaling Build
roles: Two protective frontline or utility heroes
builds.positioning: Place every supporting hero to delay enemies and create maximum attack time for the carry.
builds.items: Concentrate the best offensive items on one hero instead of dividing them between several average damage dealers.
builds.relics: Use relics that improve damage scaling, item value or the carry survival.
builds.upgrade: Upgrade the carry strongest specialization path while spending only enough resources to keep the frontline stable.
builds.alternatives: Promote a second carry when the shop stops offering useful upgrades for the original one.

builds.name: Double-Frontline Control Build
builds.positioning: Split incoming pressure between two durable heroes while keeping the damage dealer away from the initial attack.
builds.items: Distribute defensive items according to which frontline hero takes the most pressure, then place remaining damage items on the backline carry.
builds.relics: Select relics that reward longer battles, durable heroes or repeated team action.
builds.upgrade: Improve both frontline heroes enough to survive, then scale the protected damage hero.
builds.alternatives: Convert the weaker frontline slot into support when additional durability is no longer required.

builds.name: Relic-Driven Flex Build
roles: Heroes matching the strongest relic effects
builds.positioning: Position around the active relic effects rather than following a fixed formation from the beginning of the run.
builds.items: Use items to strengthen the heroes already benefiting from the relic package.
builds.relics: Build around repeated relic themes instead of selecting individually strong relics with no connection.
builds.upgrade: Commit only after two or more relics support the same strategy.
builds.alternatives: Return to a balanced team when relic effects remain scattered.

builds.name: Reserve and Pivot Build
builds.positioning: Keep the active formation simple and swap reserve heroes in when an encounter or specialization offer favors them.
builds.items: Avoid locking every valuable item onto a temporary hero before the final composition is clear.
builds.relics: Prefer flexible relics that remain useful after changing heroes.
builds.upgrade: Upgrade the stable core first and delay expensive reserve investments until a clear pivot appears.
builds.alternatives: Use reserve heroes to replace weak links instead of rebuilding the entire team.

guildrunSpecializationsAndClasses.title: Guildrun Specializations and Classes
guildrunSpecializationsAndClasses.intro: Rank upgrades can change a hero from a basic recruit into a focused tank, healer, ranged attacker, duelist, assassin, or hybrid unit. The strongest parties use complementary specializations rather than upgrading every hero toward the same type of damage.

classes.job: Hold the front line, absorb pressure, and protect fragile allies.
classes.focus: Durability, damage reduction, control, and defensive team support.
classes.multiclass: Combines well with healing or support paths that improve survival during long fights.
classes.upgrade: Defensive rank upgrades first, followed by control or team-protection effects.
classes.respec: Respec when the party already has enough defense or when later bosses require more damage.

classes.job: Restore health and keep the party stable through extended encounters.
classes.focus: Healing strength, healing frequency, protection, and team sustain.
classes.multiclass: Pairs with defensive or damage-support paths to provide value between healing actions.
classes.upgrade: Dependable healing and survival first, followed by party-wide bonuses.
classes.respec: Respec when healing exceeds the party needs or when faster battles make offensive support more valuable.

classes.job: Deal consistent damage from a safer position behind the front line.
classes.focus: Attack speed, ranged damage, target coverage, and sustained scaling.
classes.multiclass: Works well with class bonuses, attack-speed effects, and paths that improve repeated attacks.
classes.upgrade: Consistent damage upgrades before narrow effects that only work against specific targets.
classes.respec: Respec when the build lacks protection or when single-target boss damage becomes more important than general damage.

classes.job: Pressure dangerous individual enemies through strong single-target combat.
classes.focus: Direct damage, repeated attacks, survivability, and extended one-on-one pressure.
classes.multiclass: Benefits from defensive secondary paths that allow the hero to continue attacking under pressure.
classes.upgrade: Damage consistency first, followed by defensive upgrades that prevent interrupted attacks.
classes.respec: Respec when encounters contain large enemy groups or when the party needs stronger area coverage.

classes.job: Remove vulnerable or high-priority targets with concentrated burst damage.
classes.focus: Burst damage, target access, finishing power, and offensive scaling.
classes.multiclass: Pairs with flexible damage or attack-speed paths that remain useful after the opening burst.
classes.upgrade: Dependable finishing power before highly conditional damage bonuses.
classes.respec: Respec when enemies survive the opening attack or when longer fights reward sustained damage.

classes.job: Fill two party needs through multiclass combinations and flexible specialization choices.
classes.focus: Balanced damage, support, defense, or healing selected around the current party.
classes.multiclass: Provides the greatest flexibility when shops and recruitment do not offer a perfect team composition.
classes.upgrade: Choose upgrades that solve the party biggest weakness instead of splitting resources evenly.
classes.respec: Respec after major recruitment changes so the hero continues to fill a useful role.

guildrunItemsAndRelicsTierList.title: Guildrun Items and Relics Tier List
guildrunItemsAndRelicsTierList.intro: The best equipment improves an entire build instead of providing a small isolated stat increase. Flexible class bonuses, attack-speed scaling, dependable defense, healing support, and early economy effects remain useful across more heroes and difficulty levels.

tiers.bestWith: Parties containing several heroes that share or benefit from the same class direction.
tiers.reason: Class-wide scaling can improve multiple abilities and becomes stronger as the party gains ranks and specializations.

tiers.bestWith: Ranged heroes, duelists, repeated attackers, and effects triggered by frequent attacks.
tiers.reason: Attack speed increases direct damage while also activating compatible on-attack effects more often.

tiers.bestWith: Runs acquired early enough to benefit from additional shops, rerolls, recruits, and upgrades.
tiers.reason: Early economic advantages compound across the run and create more opportunities to complete powerful builds.

tiers.bestWith: Front-line heroes and parties attempting higher difficulties or Endless Mode.
tiers.reason: Dependable defense prevents failed runs and gives damage-focused heroes more time to scale.

tiers.bestWith: Dedicated healers, defensive parties, and long boss encounters.
tiers.reason: Healing improvements provide strong sustain but lose value when the party has no dependable healing source.

tiers.bestWith: Specialized ranged, duelist, or assassin builds with a clear primary damage dealer.
tiers.reason: Focused bonuses can create excellent carries but are less useful when recruitment forces a different composition.

tiers.bestWith: Tanks or heroes regularly exposed to enemy pressure.
tiers.reason: These effects are useful on the correct hero but provide limited value when placed on protected back-line units.

tiers.bestWith: Builds that can activate the relic condition consistently.
tiers.reason: Their maximum output can be strong, but missed conditions make them less consistent than universal effects.

tiers.bestWith: Temporary early-game equipment slots before stronger options appear.
tiers.reason: Small isolated bonuses are quickly replaced and rarely influence late-game specialization or Endless Mode scaling.

guildrunDifficultyAndEndless.title: Guildrun Difficulty Levels and Endless Mode
guildrunDifficultyAndEndless.intro: Higher difficulties reward efficient recruitment, controlled spending, stronger specialization synergy, and better equipment choices. Endless Mode extends the run beyond normal progression and tests how long the party can survive while building a leaderboard score.

levels.name: Difficulty 1
levels.challenge: Uses the base progression structure and introduces the complete recruitment, shop, upgrade, and boss loop.
levels.preparation: Build a balanced party with a front line, dependable damage, and enough sustain to finish the first run.

levels.name: Difficulty 2
levels.challenge: Begins the escalating difficulty path and places greater pressure on basic party construction.
levels.preparation: Avoid unnecessary rerolls and invest resources in heroes that already support the main build.

levels.name: Difficulty 3
levels.challenge: Punishes weak front lines and parties that rely on only one source of damage.
levels.preparation: Add defensive support and develop a secondary damage option before later bosses.

levels.name: Difficulty 4
levels.challenge: Requires stronger rank upgrades, specialization synergy, and more careful combat preparation.
levels.preparation: Prioritize upgrades that improve the whole party instead of spreading resources across temporary recruits.

levels.name: Difficulty 5
levels.challenge: Reduces the margin for inefficient shops, poorly matched relics, and incomplete class combinations.
levels.preparation: Plan a primary specialization direction early and keep enough resources available for important rerolls.

levels.name: Difficulty 6
levels.challenge: Demands stronger answers to boss pressure and better scaling through the middle and late stages of a run.
levels.preparation: Combine dependable survival with equipment that continues gaining value as heroes receive more upgrades.

levels.name: Difficulty 7
levels.challenge: Tests optimized multiclass combinations and exposes parties with narrow or conditional strategies.
levels.preparation: Use flexible items and relics, maintain more than one win condition, and respec heroes when the composition changes.

levels.name: Difficulty 8
levels.challenge: Represents the hardest standard difficulty and requires an efficient build from recruitment through the final boss progression.
levels.preparation: Minimize wasted purchases, complete core specialization combinations, and enter late encounters with both damage and sustain fully developed.

levels.name: Endless Mode
levels.unlock: Reached through successful progression beyond the standard run structure.
levels.challenge: Continues combat after normal boss progression while enemy pressure and long-run demands keep increasing.
levels.preparation: Favor scalable class bonuses, repeated-attack effects, strong sustain, and relic combinations that remain useful throughout an extended run.
levels.goal: Survive as long as possible and maximize the run leaderboard score.

guildrunUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Guildrun Updates and Patch Notes
guildrunUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Guildrun update coverage is organized by the part of the game affected. Players should review hero balance, specialization changes, equipment adjustments, mode updates, and bug fixes before reusing an older build or tier list.

updates.title: Hero Balance Updates
updates.changes: Tracks adjustments to hero performance, combat roles, rank upgrades, and ability behavior.
updates.impact: Damage, tank, healer, and support rankings may change when a hero receives stronger or weaker scaling.

updates.title: Specialization and Class Changes
updates.changes: Tracks specialization tuning, multiclass interactions, upgrade paths, ability keywords, and respec-related changes.
updates.impact: Existing class combinations may gain new synergies or require a different upgrade order.

updates.title: Item and Relic Adjustments
updates.changes: Tracks power changes, rarity adjustments, effect revisions, and compatibility with different heroes or classes.
updates.impact: Tier placements can move when universal effects, economy relics, or class-scaling equipment are rebalanced.

updates.title: New Heroes and Equipment
updates.changes: Covers newly added heroes, specialization options, items, relics, and related gameplay systems.
updates.impact: New additions can create stronger party roles, multiclass combinations, and late-game strategies.

updates.title: Difficulty and Endless Mode Changes
updates.changes: Tracks difficulty modifiers, boss progression, Endless Mode scaling, scoring, and leaderboard-related adjustments.
updates.impact: Recommended preparation and equipment priorities may change when high-difficulty or long-run scaling is adjusted.

updates.title: Bug Fixes and Stability
updates.changes: Covers combat errors, ability interactions, interface problems, progression issues, and general stability improvements.
updates.impact: Fixes can restore intended hero, specialization, item, or relic behavior and change results from previous builds.

updates.title: Demo and Beta Development
updates.changes: Tracks experimental features, feedback-driven revisions, demo improvements, and systems being prepared for later releases.
updates.impact: Beta changes may introduce new mechanics that affect progression strategies and future tier lists.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Guildrun

questions.question: What is Guildrun?
questions.answer: Guildrun is a single-player PvE roguelike autobattler from Leyline. You recruit heroes, develop their specialization paths, equip items and relics, then watch automatic combat clear escalating rifts.

questions.question: Is there a free Guildrun demo?
questions.answer: Yes. The Guildrun Demo is a free standalone download on Steam that covers recruitment, shop decisions, upgrades, specializations, items, relics and automatic combat before the full release.

questions.question: When is the full Guildrun release date?
questions.answer: The full version of Guildrun is targeting a 2027 release on PC via Steam. You can follow or wishlist the main Guildrun store page for future release announcements.

questions.question: How many heroes and specializations does Guildrun have?
questions.answer: Guildrun is planned around 25 recruitable heroes, 75 specialization paths and 180 specialization choices, plus more than 100 items and more than 300 relics that shape every run.

questions.question: Does Guildrun support co-op or multiplayer?
questions.answer: Co-op is part of the planned development scope for Guildrun. The current free demo is a single-player experience, with multiplayer features intended for the full release.

questions.question: What is Endless Mode in Guildrun?
questions.answer: Endless Mode extends a Guildrun run beyond normal boss progression while enemy pressure keeps increasing. It favors scalable class bonuses, repeated-attack effects and strong sustain to maximize the leaderboard score.
